task_statement:
BRICK 6-surface audit repair, Global Operating Rule 8 (goal doc commit e571947): graph_packet admission MUST reject a fan_in group whose fan_in_target_ref node is also the direct source of a fan_out group with no intervening single-target barrier node between them (fan-in and fan-out must never be the same event, per agent/skills/building-coordination/SKILL.md). P7a/P7b/P7d all burned a full real-provider Building cycle discovering this exact shape defect only at closure time, after design+work+QA had already run for real. This checker turns that expensive runtime discovery into a cheap pre-flight rejection.

Implement a new checker (support/checkers/lib/ + a check_*.py or a kernel_check registered in check_profile.py, matching existing checker conventions) that statically inspects a graph_packet plan (nodes/edges/groups, matching support/operator/plan_graph.py shapes) and rejects at admission time (before any real brick build --graph dispatch) any fan_in group whose fan_in_target_ref is the direct source of an immediately-following fan_out group with zero intervening nodes.

Negative fixture (must RED before the checker exists / on old behavior, GREEN once the checker rejects it): reconstruct the P7d (0701d) shape where docs-lane-qa and checker-lane-qa fan directly into three final-QA nodes (final-code-qa, final-axis-qa, final-evidence-qa) with no barrier. Positive fixture (must stay accepted): the P7d2/P7d3 (0701e/0701f) shape with the lane-qa-fanin-confirm barrier node between the lane-QA fan-in and the final-QA fan-out. Use the actual committed graph JSON files at project/brick-protocol/status/kernel/GOAL/brick-6-p7-easy-building-ergonomics-0701d.json (negative) and -0701f.json (positive) as real fixture material if useful.

Register the new check in check_profile.py per existing conventions (profile YAML + kernel dispatch). Do not create a second engine, a second admission path, or duplicate existing plan_graph.py validation -- extend/call from the existing graph-plan validation seam (support/operator/plan_graph.py and/or composition_graph_validate.py) where the fan_in/fan_out groups are already parsed, rather than re-parsing the graph packet from scratch.
selection_rule: Low-risk, purely additive checker task (goal doc tiering: low-risk/doc/cleanup phases may use a simpler shape than the mandatory dual-design fan-out reserved for named high-risk phases). Single design, single work, three-way final QA fan-out, closure.

nodes.work_statement: Design the fan-in/fan-out-barrier admission checker per the task statement. Read agent/skills/building-coordination/SKILL.md, support/operator/plan_graph.py, support/operator/composition_graph_validate.py, and the two fixture graph JSON files named in the task statement. Propose exact function name, file location, registration point in check_profile.py, and the negative/positive probe shape. No source edits.

Implement the checker per the accepted design. Checker-first: add the negative fixture first, prove it currently passes/is not caught (the bug exists), implement the rejection, prove the negative fixture now RED-fires as expected and the positive fixture stays accepted. Register in check_profile.py. Run py_compile, the new focused profile, and check_profile.py --all. Return changed_files and exact command output.

Attack the checker implementation: confirm the negative fixture genuinely RED-fires with the new checker (not vacuously), confirm the positive fixture (P7d2/P7d3 barrier shape) is still accepted, confirm py_compile and check_profile.py --all pass. Probe_write only, no source mutation.

Confirm this checker stays support/checker mechanics only -- it may REJECT a bad graph_packet, but must not choose Movement, targets, success, or quality; it must not become a second engine or a second admission path duplicating plan_graph.py. Probe_write only.

Confirm evidence integrity: negative/positive fixtures are real and byte-distinct from prior QA bodies, proof_limits/not_proven present, no overclaiming of coverage beyond the fan-in/fan-out-barrier shape checked. Probe_write only.

Fan in code-qa, axis-qa, evidence-qa. Report observed_evidence / narrowly_proven / not_proven / next Movement candidate. Close only if the negative fixture RED-fires and the positive fixture stays accepted with current evidence; otherwise return Link-facing transition_concern_evidence and HOLD rather than smoothing it.

proof_limits: official brick build graph evidence is support evidence only
this Building closes only Global Operating Rule 8 (fan-in/fan-out barrier admission), not whole P8/P9 readiness
not source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority
not_proven: whether this checker catches every possible malformed graph topology, only the specific fan-in-immediately-fan-out shape
P8 ship safety, P9 dynamic customer replay, fresh-machine install, real-provider reliability, customer comprehension
